page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware included with this instance type, and it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that the server delivers average multi-core performance across various compute workloads. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks both place the instance in the middle 50% tier. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core results falling into the bottom 10% and single-threaded gzip compression categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth is stable, scoring in the average tier for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations.

Given its high memory density and shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance single-core execution. It is well-suited for database operations, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and software compilation tasks, all of which achieved average tier benchmark results. However, it is less suitable for single-threaded processing or complex LLM text generation, where performance is constrained.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though the CPU resources are shared. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations, contrasted by weak single-core performance. This profile makes the instance a cost-effective option for memory-heavy workloads such as database caching, software compilation, and static web serving, while being less optimal for CPU-bound single-threaded tasks.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized KVM virtual server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixteen shared AMD EPYC cores and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for database and caching workloads.
